steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that difrasismos are often only intelligible through Aztec ceremonial culture: “semantic relations [...] can be tenuous [...] observed in Aztec ceremonial culture.” This defines the mechanism of how these metaphors work—they depend on cultural context for meaning, not inherent word relationships.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice C restates the claim that obscurity is resolved by cultural context. A reverses the passage’s point by citing semantic equivalence, while B misattributes unintelligibility to form. D conflates frequency with intelligibility, which the text does not link. That matches choice C — C is correct.
